comment: Default-FAIL verification contract. Every check starts at passes=false. A check may be flipped to true only after its verification command ran and the evidence file (log, report, or screenshot path relative to the repository root) was inspected. check-contract.(sh|ps1) refuses Done while any required check fails. Checks with required:false that have passes=false must carry a non-empty waiver_reason explaining why the check does not apply.

checks.waiver_reason: Non-code stack (JS/PY/PS1/SH guard scripts + bash test fixture) per Global Constraints 'Non-code stack' descriptor in specs/sdd-domain/tasks.md; no lint toolchain is configured for this cross-language script set. Waivable via stack: shell per risk-gate-matrix.md.
Non-code stack; the four guard twins are untyped JS/Python/PowerShell/POSIX-shell scripts with no compiled/typed toolchain applicable. Waivable via stack: shell per risk-gate-matrix.md.
Non-code stack; there is no build/compile step for the guard scripts or the bash test suite. Waivable via stack: shell per risk-gate-matrix.md.

No integration-test layer exists for this feature; the hook guard is validated end-to-end via the guard-parity fixture suite (unit-tests/acceptance-tests/regression checks) directly invoking each language twin's CLI entry point.

sdd-hook-guard is a PreToolUse hook script, not a startable dev server, Docker container, or CLI binary with an interactive entry point; its behavior is exercised directly by the guard-parity fixture suite instead.

T-006 is an additive extension (new regex/path-matcher/net-increase check) to an existing guard with no documented baseline-behavior.md; differential-test-policy.md applies to refactor/bugfix tasks with a captured baseline, which does not apply here. The Red/Green tdd evidence on the unit-tests check serves the equivalent before/after purpose.
No browser-renderable UI surface is touched; this task changes only PreToolUse hook-guard scripts and their test fixtures.
The repository's design-system/ contract governs UI-layer (rendered/visual) files; this task touches only backend guard scripts and shell test fixtures, not UI-layer files.
